loadError.title: Could not load dashboard data
loadError.bodyWithMessage: {message}. The site usually recovers on the next daily refresh.
loadError.bodyDefault: The metrics file is unreachable. The site usually recovers on the next daily refresh.

overview.sectionLede: Key metrics for the selected ASmap build
overview.noPublishedMaps: No published maps found in metrics.json.
overview.noVariantData: Build {name} has no published variant data.

buildSelector.ariaLabel: ASmap build for Overview metrics (newest builds listed first)

fallbackBadge.label: Filled fallback
fallbackBadge.tooltip: This build did not publish an unfilled variant. Numbers are read from the filled (embedded) file instead.

entries.label: Entries
info: Each entry maps an IP prefix to the autonomous system that announces it.
Read from the unfilled (source data) variant when published, falling back to the filled variant otherwise.

The vs-previous delta is only shown when both sides come from the same encoding. Filled collapses adjacent same-AS prefixes (~12 %), so a mixed comparison would report that compression as if the map had grown or shrunk. In practice this only blanks the delta on the rare filled-only build whose comparable predecessor is unfilled.
On-disk file size rides along inside the entries-chart tooltip.

uniqueAses.label: Unique ASes
info: Number of distinct autonomous systems referenced anywhere in the map.
Filled and unfilled both reference the same ASes (filled adds prefixes, never new ASes), so the number is variant-independent.

The vs-previous delta is taken against the same predecessor as the drift card, so the three cards stay aligned on which build they are comparing against. The drift card spells the predecessor's date out on its delta line.
The bar below shows the share of mapping entries that target IPv4 vs IPv6 prefixes.

drift.label: Drift vs previous
drift.filledOnlyHint: Source-data drift needs an unfilled variant.
info: Share of mapping entries that differ between this build and the most recent diffable predecessor.
Computed from unfilled-vs-unfilled diffs to isolate real source-data drift from fill-heuristic shifts.
A 5 % drift means roughly 1 in 20 lookups would now resolve to a different autonomous system.

history.sectionLede: Per-release drift and entry-count trends across every published build
history.emptyDrift: Need at least two builds and one diff to plot drift.
history.allSeriesHidden: All series hidden. Click a legend entry to bring one back.
history.noDriftData: No drift data for the picked mode.

driftCumulative.title: Cumulative Drift Since Baseline
driftCumulative.ariaLabel: Cumulative drift composition since the oldest published build. Reassigned, newly mapped, unmapped, and total drift series.
info: How far each build has wandered from the oldest published build with an unfilled variant. Lines grow over time, so a flat segment means the source data did not change against the baseline and a steep slope means it changed fast.

info.text: Prefix was mapped in both builds, but now resolves to a different autonomous system. Usually the largest slice; reflects real BGP / RPKI churn.
Prefix had no autonomous system in the baseline and now resolves to one. Reflects coverage growth as upstream data adds prefixes.
Prefix that resolved in the baseline no longer resolves. Reflects upstream data dropping prefixes, typically because RPKI / IRR coverage retracted.
Sum of the three categories. The headline distance from the baseline build, in a single number, for the build you have selected.

info: Click any legend entry to hide that line and rescale the chart to whatever stays on. Builds without an unfilled variant carry no dot and the tooltip flags them as having no diff; the line bridges across so the trend stays readable.

driftStep.title: Drift Between Builds
driftStep.ariaLabel: Step drift composition between each build and its last diffable predecessor. Reassigned, newly mapped, unmapped, and total drift series.
info: How much each build changed compared to the previous build with an unfilled variant. Each release renders as one stacked bar: a tall bar is a release that moved a lot of prefixes, a flat bar is a quiet release.

info.text: Prefix was mapped in both builds, but now resolves to a different autonomous system between the two sides.
Prefix that was unmapped in the previous build now resolves to an autonomous system.
Prefix that resolved in the previous build no longer resolves.
Height of the full stack. Matches the "drift vs previous" number on the overview card for the same pair.

info: Click any legend entry to hide that category and rescale. Builds without an unfilled variant have no comparable previous build and render as an empty column; the diff is taken against the last build that actually published an unfilled variant.

entriesChart.title: Source Data Entries Over Time
entriesChart.ariaLabel: ASmap entries count over time, embedded vs source data variant. Hover the chart for exact values per build.
entriesChart.noPublishedVariants: No published variants for the loaded builds.
info: Number of prefix-to-AS entries each ASmap build carries. Tracks coverage growth in absolute terms, alongside the per-release growth bars below.

info.text: Raw upstream prefix entries the build was produced from. Grows monotonically as new prefixes are advertised by upstream routing data.

Entries after the fill heuristic collapses adjacent same-AS prefixes into single rows. Consistently lower than unfilled; the gap between the two lines visualises the heuristic's compression at a glance.

info: Hover any build for the entry counts, the matching on-disk size in MB, and the fill-compression ratio between the two variants. Builds that did not publish a variant carry no dot and the tooltip names them as not published, but the line connects the surrounding points so the trend stays readable.

mapDeltaChart.title: Source Data Entries Delta Between Builds
mapDeltaChart.ariaLabel: Source-data entry count delta between each ASmap build and its last diffable predecessor. Hover each bar for details.
mapDeltaChart.empty: Need at least two builds with an unfilled variant to plot deltas.
info: Gain or loss in source-data prefix entries between each build and the most recent earlier build that published an unfilled variant.

A positive bar means this build added that many real (prefix to ASN) entries on top of its diffable predecessor. A negative bar means entries fell out of the upstream data, typically because RPKI / IRR coverage retracted for some prefix.

Computed from the unfilled (source data) variant of both sides. Builds whose immediate predecessor is filled-only fall back to the last build that did publish an unfilled variant, so a single filled-only release no longer blanks out the surrounding bars. Builds without an unfilled variant themselves still render as an empty column.
Hover any bar for the exact entry counts and the date of the predecessor the diff was taken against.

diff.needTwoUnfilled: Need at least two builds with an unfilled variant to compare.

results.samePair: Pick two different maps to see what changed.
results.unavailable: No precomputed diff for this pair. One of the two builds is missing its unfilled (source data) variant.
info: Each entry-level change between Map A and Map B falls into exactly one of three buckets.

info.text: A prefix was mapped in both Map A and Map B, but now resolves to a different autonomous system. This is where most ASmap edits land.
A prefix had no autonomous system in Map A and now resolves to one in Map B.
A prefix that resolved to an autonomous system in Map A no longer resolves to one in Map B.

info: The caption under each bucket splits the count by address family. Bitcoin Core peer diversity treats IPv4 and IPv6 as separate dimensions, so a diff that moves 10 000 IPv4 prefixes has a different operational signature than one that moves 10 000 IPv6 prefixes.

info.text: The footer line under the stacked bar tracks how many distinct autonomous systems each build maps, plus how many appeared (present in Map B but not in Map A) and disappeared (present in A but not in B). Appeared − disappeared equals the "+N vs previous" delta on the Unique ASes overview card for the same pair — the two views agree on the same arithmetic.

info: Computed from the unfilled (source data) variant of each build, so each change is a real BGP / RPKI / IRR shift rather than a fill-heuristic artefact.

topMovers.title: Top Movers
topMovers.empty: No top movers in the selected diff.
topMovers.noMatches: No autonomous systems match this filter.
info: Autonomous systems most affected by the selected diff, ranked by entry-level change count.

info.text: Number of prefix to ASN entries in the binary trie that name this AS on either side of the diff. Each entry is counted once.

How much of this AS's presence the diff visited, expressed as a multiple of its prefix count. 0.50× means roughly half of its prefixes participated; 1.00× means the diff touched as many trie positions as the AS holds in either map. Values above 1.00× appear when one build aggregates the AS into a single large block while the other splits the same range into many smaller pieces — the diff then visits many trie positions per leaf, so the ratio is not capped to 100 %.
This AS's share of the total entry-level changes between the two builds.
↗ gained from the counterpart, ↘ lost to it, ↔ prefixes moved both ways, → unmapped means the prefixes lost their ASN entirely.

filter.placeholder: Filter by AS number or operator

tooltip.unmapped: prefixes no longer resolve to any ASN
